rulings.Case Title: PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA VS. PROPERTY SUBJECT TO DISPOSITION $586.00 U.S. ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Order For Terminating Sanctions And Striking Claim (15-006)
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, October 18, 2018, Line 1, PETITIONER PEOPLE OF THE STATE OF CALIFORNIA'S Motion For Order For Terminating Sanctions And Striking Claim (15-006) Pro Tem Judge Aaron Minnis, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to aaron@minnisandsmallets.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: CHRISTINE WEOHLER VS. CITY AND COUNTY OF SAN FRANCISCO
rulings.Calendar Matter: Motion For Issuance Of Writ Of Mandate
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, October 18, 2018, Line 10, Motion For Issuance Of Writ Of Mandate The petition for writ of mandate is DENIED in part and GRANTED in part. Substantial evidence supports the hearing officer's determination that the dog Buster is vicious and dangerous. (See SF Health Code §42 et seq.) However, the health code requires consideration of alternative remedies to killing such a dog. (Id. at §42.3.) The remedy of transferring ownership of Buster to a person in a rural locale was raised at the administrative hearing, but not addressed in the hearing officer's "statement of decision." Buster's owner shall have until November 2, 2018, to effect transfer of Buster to a rural locale. A status hearing is hereby set for November 2 in Department 302. =302/rbu

rulings.Case Title: HOEM & ASSOCIATES INC., A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION VS. UNIVERSITY OF THE PACIFIC ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, October 18, 2018, Line 3, CROSS DEFENDANT ARMSTRONG FLOORING, INC., A PENNSYLVANIA's DEMURRER TO CROSS COMPLAINT Cross-defendant Armstrong Flooring, Inc.'s demurrer is OVERRULED. Armstrong argues that it "owes no duty" to other component manufacturers. However, the cross-complaint is for equitable indemnity and, since American Motorcycle Ass'n v. Sup. Ct. (1977) 20 Cal.3d 578, that is often the case when this cause of action is pled. Armstrong also claims uncertainty about what it is sued for, but the cross-complaint is adequate, particularly given its incorporation by reference of other cross-complaints in the litigation. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Maxxon Corporation is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/rbu

rulings.Case Title: WAYNE RUDEN VS. C.R. BARD, INC., A NEW JERSEY CORPORATION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO ANSWER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, October 18, 2018, Line 4, DEFENDANTs C.R. BARD, INC., and BARD PERIPHERAL VASCULAR, INC. DEMURRER TO ANSWER TO 3RD AMENDED COMPLAINT The Bard defendants' demurrer to the fourth amended complaint (FAC) is OVERRULED. First, the FAC adequately pleads fraud by nondisclosure (a/k/a concealment). No plaintiff-defendant relationship is required. Rather, (1) intentionally failing to disclose facts known to defendant that plaintiff could not have discovered and/or (2) disclosing some facts to plaintiff but intentionally failing to disclose other facts making the disclosures deceptive are both long-established other bases for this kind of fraud. (See CACI 1901 and cases cited in its use notes.) The Fourth District opinion Bigler-Engler v Berg, Inc. (2017) 7 Cal.App.5th 276 does not grapple with this precedent from other districts and our supreme court. In any event, even Bigler-Engler states that "an affirmative statement may be so misleading that it may give rise to a fraud cause of action even where the relationship or transaction would be insufficient to give rise to a generalized duty to disclose." (Id. at 312-13.) Such affirmative statements are pled here. Second, fraud is pled with sufficient particularity. Less specificity is required when defendant possesses full information and plaintiff does not. (Committee on Children's Television, Inc. v. General Foods Corp. (1983) 35 Cal.3d 197, 217.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/rbu

rulings.Case Title: VIRAJ PATEL VS. BARRY EDWARD O'NEILL ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff'S Notice Of Motion And Motion To Compel Surveillance Materials (Responses To Requests For Production, Set Two, And To Special Interrogatories, Set Two)
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, October 18, 2018, Line 8, PLAINTIFF VIRAJ PATEL's Motion To Compel Surveillance Materials (Responses To Requests For Production, Set Two, And To Special Interrogatories, Set Two) As is common in personal injury litigation, defendants' counsel surveilled plaintiff, who is said to have lost his sight in a motor vehicle accident. Plaintiff seeks to compel a deposition of the investigator as well as surveillance photos, videos and/or reports and interrogatory answers regarding the surveillance. The attorney-client privilege and attorney work product doctrine are at issue. Plaintiff says the investigator is "an employee of defense counsel" - an assertion not helpful to plaintiff, given the attorney-client privilege's breadth. Actually, the investigator is more appropriately viewed - as defendants do - as an expert consultant who may or may not testify at trial. Such consultants are not subject to deposition unless and until they are designated trial witnesses. (See Williamson v. Sup. Ct. (1978) 21 Cal.3d 829, 834-35.) The surveillance photos, videos and/or reports are a closer call. In Coito v. Sup. Ct. (2012) 54 Cal.4th 480, 486, our supreme court held that recorded witness statements are entitled to protection under the work product doctrine. I find the Coito logic to apply to surveillance recordings and reports too, because they may "reveal¿attorney's impressions, conclusions, opinions, or legal research or theories." (See id.) (The opinion that plaintiff relies on - Suezaki v. Sup. Ct. (1962) 58 Cal.2d 166 - is less persuasive, as it pre-dates modern discovery law.) Coito calls for a finding that counsel's work product would be revealed. (54 Cal. 4th at 486.) I therefore order submission on or before October 22, 2018, of any and all surveillance photos, videos and reports for my in camera review. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =302/rbu

rulings.Case Title: YA-HSIANG CHEN HU VS. YOU HAO CHEN ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Third Amended Complaint
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, October 18, 2018, Line 5, PLAINTIFF YA-HSIANG HU Notice Of Motion And Motion For Leave To File Third Amended Complaint Plaintiff Hu's motion for leave to file a third amended complaint is GRANTED. California policy is to liberally allow such amendments. (Mesler v. Bragg Mgmt. Co (1985) 39 Cal.3d 290, 296.) As no trial date is set, prejudice is minimal. (See Morgan v. Sup. Ct. (1959) 172 Cal.App.2d 527, 530.) Leave to amend is denied for futility only when the futility is patent, such as when a settlement or statute of limitations bars the claim. (Vaillette v. Fireman's Fund Ins. Co. (1993) 18 Cal.App.4th 680, 685.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. Counsel for Plaintiff is required to prepare a proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring it to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the tentative ruling is not contested. =302/rbu

rulings.Case Title: TIAN ZHANG VS. APEX CONSTRUCTION ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, October 18, 2018, Line 7, CROSS DEFENDANT BOSCO LAI's MOTION TO STRIKE CROSS COMPLAINT CCP §430.41 requires counsel to "meet and confer in person or by telephone" before filing a demurrer. Attorney Kim's declaration indicates that did not occur here. Further, my review of the demurrer finds that counsel should be able to resolve many, if not all, of their issues with a meet-and-confer. Thus, the demurrer and its associated motion to strike are ordered OFF CALENDAR to give counsel an opportunity to comply with §430.41. The parties are ordered to meet and confer and Lai's time to file a responsive pleading is extended to November 19, 2018. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =302/rbu
